content.problemStatement:
You can perform the following operation on an array AAA:


Choose 1‚Č§i<‚ą£A‚ą£1 \le i \lt |A|1‚Č§i<‚ą£A‚ą£ such that Ai‚Č†Ai+1A_i \ne A_{i + 1}Ai‚ÄčÓÄ†=Ai+1‚Äč, and delete both AiA_iAi‚Äč and Ai+1A_{i + 1}Ai+1‚Äč from the array (renumbering the elements after i+1i + 1i+1).
Define f(A)f(A)f(A) as f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1 if the array AAA can be changed into an empty array by deleting all elements using the above operation any number of times, otherwise f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0.

Define g(A)g(A)g(A) on array AAA of size NNN as ‚ąĎL=1N‚ąĎR=LNf(A[L,R])\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} f(A[L,R])‚ąĎL=1N‚Äč‚ąĎR=LN‚Äčf(A[L,R]) where A[L,R]A[L,R]A[L,R] represents the subarray of AAA from the LthL^{th}Lth to the RthR^{th}Rth index, i.e. [AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äč].

Given integers NNN and MMM, compute ‚ąĎg(A)\sum g(A)‚ąĎg(A) over all arrays AAA of length NNN and satisfying 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \le A_i \le M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M. Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the required sum modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM both do not exceed 10610^6106.

Explanation:
Test case 111 : g([1,2])=1g([1, 2]) = 1g([1,2])=1 as f([1])=0,f([2])=0f([1]) = 0, f([2]) = 0f([1])=0,f([2])=0 and f([1,2])=1f([1, 2]) = 1f([1,2])=1. In general, one can verify g([x,y])=1g([x, y]) = 1g([x,y])=1 if and only if x‚Č†yx \ne yxÓÄ†=y and 000 otherwise. There are 10‚čÖ910 \cdot 910‚čÖ9 ways to choose x,yx, yx,y such that x‚Č†yx \ne yxÓÄ†=y and 1‚Č§x,y‚Č§101 \le x, y \le 101‚Č§x,y‚Č§10.
Test case 222 : It can be shown that all arrays always have g(A)=0g(A) = 0g(A)=0.
